rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of demonstrating that collateral estoppel applies to bar plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant. Plaintiffs failed to create a triable issue whether collateral estoppel applies. The federal court¿s decision in Gottschall v. General Electric Co. (E.D. PA No. 2:11-CV-60035-ER) granting General Dynamics Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment bars plaintiffs¿ claims against Crane Co. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HARVEY NULL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Industrial Electric Service Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his claims against defendant. By failing to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s General Order 129 interrogatory responses are sustained. Interrogatory responses may only be used against the responding party. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ROBARE VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant Century Lumber Centers Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 6, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Specially appearing defendant Century Lumber Center¿s motion to quash service of summons is continued to June 19, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503 per agreement of the parties. = (503/TLJ)
